summary: The proposed coverage assessment keeps the registry-surveillance repair within a triage-summary synthesis boundary: it names deferred fisetin frailty registry candidates, explicitly states that ClinicalTrials.gov snapshots were not fetched, avoids adding unsupported extraction-grade no-results records, and keeps active-trial, review-landscape, and cross-human safety gaps open.

findings.claim_or_issue: The active-trials registry category is marked thin and the known gap names deferred fisetin frailty registries NCT03430037 and NCT03675724 while preserving the failed ClinicalTrials.gov snapshot ingestion blocker.
findings.why_it_matters: The candidate must not convert registry candidates into source-backed no-results evidence without retained registry snapshots.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; keep the deferred registry candidates as a surveillance gap until source snapshots are fetched.

findings.claim_or_issue: The assessment summary and provenance retain triage_summary or abstract/full-text-specific maturity labels instead of upgrading the unfetched registry candidates to extraction-grade records.
findings.why_it_matters: Synthesis should remain bounded by available snapshots and should not imply that unavailable registry records were reviewed.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; maintain the stated source-snapshot requirement before adding new registry status records.

findings.claim_or_issue: The proposed assessment continues to state that systematic-review screening, full article/table extraction for several human studies, and harmonized cross-human adverse-event synthesis remain incomplete.
findings.why_it_matters: These caveats prevent the adequate vertical-slice coverage verdict from being read as a complete clinical efficacy or safety synthesis.
findings.recommended_action: No revision required for this lane; leave these limitations visible until dedicated extraction and review-landscape jobs close them.
